Governing Equation(s): F_b = (rho_air - rho_liftgas) * V * g; M_payload = F_net / g; N_modules = floor(M_payload / M_per_module)
Key Inputs - Fixed or Parameter: Platform spec (name and source); rho_air = 1.225 kg/m3; rho_H2 = 0.0899 kg/m3; g = 9.81 m/s2; LHV_H2 = 120 MJ/kg
Output: Feasible m_dot range (kg/hr); maximum module count N; hydrogen offtake energy vs. buoyancy cost curve
Self-Sufficiency Role: Sets throughput ceiling for all downstream modules

Governing Equation(s): Q_net = Q_reaction - Q_loss - Q_preheat_net; Q_reaction = m_dot * HHV * eta_HTC; Q_loss = U * A * (T_reactor - T_ambient); Q_preheat = m_dot * moisture * cp_water * (T_reactor - T_feed); Q_preheat_net = Q_preheat * (1 - epsilon)
Key Inputs - Swept: moisture fraction (0.40 to 0.80); m_dot (kg/hr) across range from Module 1
Key Inputs - Fixed or Parameter: T_reactor (deg C - design parameter); eta_HTC (from literature); HHV (kJ/kg dry - from literature); U (W/m2K - estimated); A_reactor (m2 - from Module 3 geometry); epsilon (from Module 4); T_ambient (deg C); T_feed (deg C); cp_water = 4.18 kJ/kgK
Output: Q_net (kW) as function of moisture and m_dot; crossover moisture threshold where Q_net = 0; minimum eta_HTC for self-sufficiency at each moisture level

Governing Equation(s): sigma_hoop = P * r / t; t_required = P * r / (sigma_allowable * eta_weld); M_vessel = rho_steel * 2 * pi * r * L * t (cylindrical shell); end caps add approx 15-20% to shell mass
Key Inputs - Swept: T_reactor (deg C) swept from 180 to 250 to derive P_autogenous via steam tables
Key Inputs - Fixed or Parameter: r - inner radius (m - design parameter); L - vessel length (m - design parameter); sigma_allowable (Pa) = sigma_yield / safety factor; sigma_yield approx 205 MPa for 316 SS; safety factor 3 to 4 for pressure vessel service; eta_weld = 0.85 (radiographed weld); rho_steel = 8000 kg/m3
Output: Wall thickness t (mm) as function of T_reactor; vessel mass M_vessel (kg) as function of T_reactor; updated module mass feeding back into Module 1 N_modules calculation
Self-Sufficiency Role: Constrains module mass budget; higher T increases vessel mass and reduces module count

Governing Equation(s): epsilon = Q_actual / Q_max; Q_max = C_min * (T_hot_in - T_cold_in); C = m_dot * cp for each stream; C_min = min(C_hot - C_cold); Q_recovered = epsilon * C_min * (T_hot_in - T_cold_in)
Key Inputs - Fixed or Parameter: m_dot (kg/hr - from Module 1); T_hot_in (deg C - approx T_reactor); T_cold_in (deg C - approx 15 to 30); cp_water = 4.18 kJ/kgK for both streams (first-pass assumption)
Output: Q_recovered (kW) as function of epsilon; minimum epsilon required to close energy balance; delta Q_net between epsilon = 0 (no recovery baseline) and epsilon = 0.70 (realistic); Q_preheat_net returned to Module 2
Self-Sufficiency Role: Reduces Q_preheat term in Module 2; determines heat exchanger design requirement

Governing Equation(s): Q_net >= W_pump + W_auxiliary; W_pump = m_dot * delta_P / (rho_slurry * eta_pump); delta_P dominated by P_autogenous(T_reactor); delta_P_friction = f * (L/D) * (rho * v^2 / 2)
Key Inputs - Swept: moisture fraction (0.40 to 0.80); m_dot (kg/hr) across feasible range; T_reactor (deg C) as parameter shift; epsilon as parameter shift
Key Inputs - Fixed or Parameter: eta_pump = 0.65 (slurry service estimate); rho_slurry = 950 to 1100 kg/m3; pipe L/D (design parameter - assumed compact skid layout); W_auxiliary = 5 to 10 percent of Q_net (placeholder); f from Moody chart or Colebrook equation
Output: 2D feasibility surface in moisture x m_dot space showing self-sufficient operating region; boundary contour where Q_net = W_pump + W_auxiliary; sensitivity maps for T_reactor and epsilon
Self-Sufficiency Role: Primary deliverable — defines the viable operating envelope of the system
